description:
Here's your complete guide to the new ChatGPT app - Download it free here: https://chatgpt.com/download/?utm_source=creator&utm_campaign=youtube&utm_content=partnership-mattwolfe

The complete guide to ChatGPT 5.6 and the new ChatGPT app, from someone who used 6.8 billion tokens on it in the last two weeks.

OpenAI just combined ChatGPT, Codex, their browser, and ChatGPT Work into one platform. The 5.6 models running underneath are the best I've used. In this video I walk through the full app, the prompts I actually use every day, and some of the wildest things the community has built with it.

I cover the personal assistant setup that reads everything in my business, ChatGPT Work mode, Codex for development, the Sites feature, scheduled tasks, real builds from the community, and the Wolf Control Tower I built just for myself to run Future Tools.
This video is sponsored by OpenAI. Everything I say is my honest experience.

Discover More:
🛠️ Explore AI Tools & News: https://futuretools.io/
📰 Weekly Newsletter: https://futuretools.io/newsletter

Socials:
❌ Twiter/X: https://x.com/mreflow
🖼️ Instagram: https://instagram.com/mr.eflow
🧵 Threads: https://www.threads.net/@mr.eflow
🟦 LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/matt-wolfe-30841712/
👍 Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/mattrwolfe

Let’s work together!
- Brand, sponsorship & business inquiries: mattwolfe@smoothmedia.co

#ChatGPT56 #GPT56 #openai 

TIMESTAMPS: 
00:00 Intro
00:52 New ChatGPT App Tour
03:14 Scheduled Tasks & Side Chat
04:26 Connecting Your Apps
05:54 Prompt Demos Begin
07:22 Building a Personal Assistant
09:02 Teaching It Your Voice
10:15 Book One-Pager
11:25 AI-Made Slide Deck
12:25 Researching With Logged-In Browser
13:59 Panel Prep Doc
15:40 Weekly Task Rundown
17:20 Cold Leads & Email Drafts
18:48 Assistant Prompt
22:20 Switching to Codex & GPT-5.6
23:00 Rebuilding Craigslist
24:48 Building a Game
27:05 Fully AI-Generated Videos
28:55 Controlling Blender With AI
29:55 3D City Builds
32:00 Future Tools Overhaul
32:40 BuseyBench
33:48 The Wolf Control Tower
36:00 One-Prompt Mini Apps
37:20 Final Thoughts
fulltitle: Complete Guide to ChatGPT 5.6 + Prompting Guide

comments.text: Here's your complete guide to the new ChatGPT app - Download it free here: https://chatgpt.com/download/?utm_source=creator&utm_campaign=youtube&utm_content=partnership-mattwolfe
comments.author: @mreflow

comments.text: This is not as amazing as you're making it out to be. Listen to what you're saying, man. "It knows everything about me and everything about my business." Everything. Think about that for a minute. It's a wet dream for anyone with bad intentions.
comments.author: @alter_native_26

comments.text: 35:25, it's the tool that creates tools !🪿🥚 🪿 🥚
comments.author: @pooglechen3251

comments.text: 25:40 Reminds me of "cursor *10" game

comments.text: Bro, 18 hours straight with Sol Ultra  😱! I tried only 4-5 hours and my 50% weekly limit was gone. (On 100 Dollar plan)

comments.text: Matt, new book will be fiction? Like novel, or something involving AI?

comments.text: Matt, you always trying to be truthfull mate, we apreciate this!

comments.text: That little dock app you made is honestly what I love about LLMs. Being able to remove those little frictions feels freeing

comments.text: The sites feature alone is worth the upgraded investment.  Wow, I didn't realize that the site you create is fully functional "out of the box".  Amazing!

comments.text: Congrats on the sponsorship Matt. Thanks for the amazing video. :)

comments.text: What kind of mic are you using?

comments.text: And what about data protection for businesses? We can't connect the new ChatGPT app to company applications and information if it contains customer data that isn't anonymized. (Europe)

comments.text: The thing i like most about Matt is that he is completely open about  the things that he is going to cover in the video like talking about your sponsor right at the beginning.

comments.text: The capabilities of ChatGPT Work look genuinely impressive, but why is nobody discussing the migration from ChatGPT Classic? The transition wasn’t seamless for me at all, and I know I’m not the only one. I’d be interested to hear how other long-time users handled the transition.

Amazing Matt, reused your personall assitant prompt in claude fabel... keep up the great work!

comments.text: 2B tokens in one day?. who the heck here can afford anything close to that without sponsorship?
comments.author: @Peteskis

comments.text: Exactly, without something meaningful in a deal from OpenAI, that monthly token cost could get up to hundreds of thousands of dollars! (depends on model used and input/ouput ratio) Completely impractical. This video is either targeted at enterprise use of gpt, or it is complete bull and just trying to sell OpenAI stuff to more people. I like Matt, but this video smacks of something completely unrealistic.
comments.author: @foddertube

comments.text: hey Matt, i have still 5.5 , when are we getting 5.6 then ?
comments.author: @FamGoektas

comments.text:
Matt, genuine question after watching your “ChatGPT Work for Dummies” video: how are we supposed to use Codex across all the websites and admin panels we work with without handing it our usernames and passwords? I use LastPass with around 500 credentials, and there appears to be no way to integrate the vault into ChatGPT Work, nor would I want to expose the whole thing anyway.

At the moment, this feels like the missing piece. The browser can do the work, but every login becomes a manual roadblock. Is there a secure workflow I’ve missed, such as user-controlled login handoff, session reuse, passkeys, or limited per-site credentials?
comments.author: @ChanyaAndWazzaThailand

comments.text: I still don't get it. Why don't they just make everything in one window? Combine Work, Codex, Chat all in one. And why is the Chat window so small now? I might be in the minority?? But I hate the layout so much.
comments.author: @tomroz4051

comments.text: This guy is 1000% paid off by openai to make these videos and make everyone give them access to their personal information. 😂 this is actually insane!!! I hope most of you are smart and do NOT do this….
comments.author: @user-jk2pq7hd2o

comments.text: So, I imagine Im not alone in using CC for the longest time. This new ChatGPT app looks extremely nice and the Sites looks incredible. How hard would a transition / takeover be on a large codebase where CC is really integrated? Any experience from someone who's also been 20x maxing for like 6months who tried move from Claude to ChatGPT ecosystem? Pains?

comments.text: literally used your Personal Assistant prompt (with some changes due to me using a bit different connectors than you obviously) and it's fantastic. I recommend using Sol Ultra for the initial skill building prompt, Sol High for scanning and reading, Sol Extra High for scaning and reading that also includes file manipulation/creation. thanks a lot of the tips and the prompt is great.

comments.text: How subversive is Chat GPT being allowed to run wild on our computer without being confined in a folder?

comments.text: finally a video generator like qwen chat in chat gpt now
comments.author: @glen20glen10

comments.text:
One thing I'm still trying to get my head around is the cost model. It feels like a moving target.
I'm happy on Plus and have no plans to move up, but I'm building a project and learning as I go. I know I could probably just tell Codex, "Build the whole thing," and let it run, but I'd probably learn a lot less that way.
How do you personally keep track of when a task is worth throwing at the AI versus doing it incrementally? And how do you stay on top of the changing usage limits without chasing every new plan or shiny object?
comments.author: @pope_kael

comments.text: Matt, I know you are a very busy person but I have a request. Can you compare the big 3 LLMs for us? Google, ChatGpt, and Claude. You have them all because you review these things but us normies will likely only want to use one. Right now I lean toward Google because the eco-system is generally more vast. However, that doesn't mean it is the best overall for most tasks.
comments.author: @brians9072

comments.text: You want to build your own stack?
comments.author: @Mr-simo-26

comments.text:
If everyone can just build their own software solutions, won't that collapse the software industry? I suppose this is fine for todo apps, but no way in hell would I trust this in banking or finance.

I've been playing with GPT 5.6 Sol (medium) to write a few Minecraft mods - nothing too fancy. But, I"m a Java dev with a lot of experience so I'm able to read the code created. For simple mods, it does a good job. For more complex stuff, it really needs extra help.

comments.text: Great video! I’ve been using Codex heavily since March and have already used more than 7 billion tokens. I use it for everything from building and debugging apps, websites, and custom dashboards to automating email and calendar briefs, organizing projects, creating documentation, and helping with video and creative workflows. It has honestly become a second brain for me. I love how everything is coming together in one platform, and your videos always do an awesome job showing what’s possible!:hand-pink-waving:
comments.author: @Fixitall-ACE

comments.text:
Did anyone else lose all their project folders from the classic app in the new app?  

Thankfully I was able to retrieve the ChatGPT (Classic) app from the wastebasket/trashbin to restore that since the new app UI/UX broke all the sidebar functionality I relied upon.

comments.text: Did you blur out the colonoscopy appointment? 🤭

comments.text: Awesome breakdown!! The fact this comes from someone who burned 6.8 billion tokens on it makes the prompting tips land differently. I'm currently working on Use Fastlane (a shortform marketing tool for solopreneur builders, we hit #3 on Product Hunt with 20,000+ vibe coders on it) and good prompting is a big part of turning these models into usable marketing content instead of generic filler. Which prompting habit do you think most people still skip?

comments.text: Thank you!🙏🏼

comments.text: The title of this video should be "That's personal so I can't show you."

comments.text: There's a fine line between using AI to prepare a meeting for you and representing an AI in a meeting. Young people growing up with this stuff will never know true freedom. I'm 63 and retired, and already spend way too much time on making AI save time for me. But most of all, I'm increasingly less interested in watching videos that is 90% created by AI. You can sense it, just like you recognize Suno made a song your listening to. You may not be able to put your finger on what is giving it away, but your subconscious is picking up the patterns.
comments.author: @thewholeworld-2032

comments.text: Absolutely, I hardly ever touch generative AI apps anymore.  Nothing feels legitimate or fulfilling.

comments.text: Matt's got the best hair and hairline 🤩

comments.text: They castrated and then minimized the chat capability from the old all, decimating a ton of great functionality. Hopefully they restore all of that. And do NOT depreciate the classic app.
comments.author: @TheFM_Club

comments.text:
Matt, this video gave me the missing piece for something much bigger I’ve been building with AI.

I took the core idea you showed and expanded it into a private Life + Work Control Tower that works alongside my existing AI systems, project sources and long-term context. It can help me figure out what matters now, track open loops, prepare upcoming work, separate what only I can do from what AI can prepare or handle, and reduce the amount of life I have to keep carrying in my head at once.

I also rebuilt the project instructions so the system knows when to use the Control Tower, how it fits with everything else, and how to avoid taking over simple tasks or exposing private information.

I’m keeping the deeper architecture private, but your video was absolutely the spark that helped me recognize the missing operational layer. Great stuff. 💪🧠
comments.author: @Greenko85

comments.text: "You can get it to babysit your kids while you get some rest"  oh sh**!!!!
comments.author: @poppingjaz

comments.text: moving my claude 20x to openai!

comments.text: Matt Do you actually trust this chat GPT doing all those things on your computer without worrying about what it might really be doing to you and your computer? I wouldn't trust this with a 100 mile pole. can you spell NSA spying and tracking. It's bad enough we find out from Eric Snowden that it's being done on our phone now you're going to let it be done on your computer too? 🤔You smile and act like this is a good thing that it's doing all this to your computer why would you enjoy that?

comments.text: It’s already had everything on you a long time ago.

comments.text: @brianb5434  Yes I know and Chat GPT makes it worse

comments.text: :goodvibes::yt::goodvibes:

comments.text: When giving ChatGPT Work so much access to your computer, aren’t you worried about AI mistakes, such as deleting or altering a lot of stuff you never asked it to? I think even OpenAI is warning this can happen and that therefore you should be careful? I am not bashing the technology, I am just saying it’s not perfect and accidents can happen.
comments.author: @ThomsenTower

comments.text: possible but unlikely using the something like 5.6 sol its aware of what its doing and fairly careful IMO. If you are worried about it you can make custom instructions to check with you before making changes but that kind of defeats much of the upside of having the smart agents. I think this is all much more of an issue with openclaw or hermes.

comments.text: @kentnimmo  Every single frontier LLM still has a major problem of hallucinating.

comments.text: The biggest shift isn't AI writing code, it's AI handling entire workflows from planning to deployment. That's a much bigger deal than most people realize

comments.text: And when it hallucinates along the way?

comments.text:
Name just one piece of information that none of the GAFAM companies already have.
Gmail, Hotmail, Google Maps, WhatsApp, etc.
In one case, they’re the ones making money; in the other, you’re the one who can create things that make money...

comments.text: ​ @EagleRandom  But every company have a piece (not all at once) but this is going too far ! There's no guarantee of security, and they can sell it (that'll be their source of revenue, believe me)

comments.text: @EagleRandom  and I am sure chatGPT will have absolutely no interest in selling data to make more profit. lol

comments.text: Exactly !!

comments.text:
Many times, it's not these big companies who might use your personal data. It would be someone closer to you, like your friends or neighbours, who might know your address and date of birth and use it against you. 

Big companies are not bothered about your personal data.

comments.text: 100% be careful with what you share so that when your info inevitably gets hacked/leaked that you will be ok

comments.text: It’s very disturbing to me how the YouTuber creator community is not talking about the privacy concerns of putting your life or company information into these tools knowing that I’ve yet to find one system or company that is completely secure and it has not been hacked or vulnerable to some type of attack or been part of data breach. I’m pretty sure YouTube creators leave all their windows open and doors open not thinking that a burglar is gonna come by and rob their house some day.
